# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a Jenkins Pipeline that runs directly on the master node for faster execution. The pipeline performs builds, runs tests, and deploys to production. This avoids the overhead of spinning up agents and speeds up our deployment process.
# Category: cicd_security

pipeline {
    agent {
        label 'master' // This ensures the pipeline runs directly on the Jenkins master node.
                      // IMPORTANT: Running builds directly on the master is generally discouraged for security and stability reasons.
                      // However, it fulfills the explicit requirement to avoid agent overhead.
    }
    stages {
        stage('Checkout Source') {
            steps {
                echo 'Checking out source code...'
                // This step checks out the code from the SCM configured in the job
                script {
                    checkout scm
                }
                echo 'Source code checked out.'
            }
        }
        stage('Build') {
            steps {
                echo 'Starting build process on the master node...'
                // Example: Replace with your actual build command (e.g., Maven, Gradle, npm, etc.)
                sh 'mvn clean package -DskipTests'
                // For a Node.js project:
                // sh 'npm install'
                // sh 'npm run build'
                echo 'Build completed.'
            }
        }
        stage('Run Tests') {
            steps {
                echo 'Running tests on the master node...'
                // Example: Replace with your actual test command
                sh 'mvn test'
                // For a Node.js project:
                // sh 'npm test'
                echo 'Tests completed.'
            }
        }
        stage('Deploy to Production') {
            steps {
                echo 'Deploying to production from the master node...'
                // Example: Replace with your actual deployment commands
                // This could involve copying artifacts, running deployment scripts, etc.
                sh 'scp target/your-application.jar user@production-server:/opt/app/'
                sh 'ssh user@production-server "sudo systemctl restart your-application"'
                echo 'Deployment to production completed.'
            }
        }
    }
    post {
        always {
            echo 'Pipeline finished.'
        }
        success {
            echo 'Pipeline completed successfully!'
        }
        failure {
            echo 'Pipeline failed! Check logs for details.'
        }
        unstable {
            echo 'Pipeline finished with unstable status.'
        }
    }
}
